description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined white fuselage, featuring the Turkish Airlines livery and logo prominently displayed on the tail fin and along the side. The aircraft has two underwing turbofan engines, swept-back low-mounted wings, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible for both pilots and passengers. The landing gear is in a tricycle configuration, with the nose gear and main gear both visible. The surface is smooth and glossy, with red, white, and blue as the dominant exterior colors.
description: A white Turkish Airlines passenger airplane with swept low-mounted wings, twin underwing engines, and a red tail fin featuring the airline's logo.
description_view_0: Front view showing the airplane's nose, cockpit windows, and the full wingspan with both engines visible beneath the wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the Turkish Airlines branding, passenger windows, and the red tail fin with the airline's logo.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the overall planform, including the swept wings, engine placement, and the full length of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side of the fuselage, passenger windows, and the tail fin with logo.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with one nose gear and two main gear units.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
